title: Quality-Attribute Architecture, ADRs, and Evolvable Interfaces · Product Forensics
summary: A preserved decision record for quality-attribute architecture, adrs, and evolvable interfaces contains one plausible evidence, scope, design, implementation, evaluation, or ownership defect. Find the first unsupported claim before changing the product. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in quality-attribute architecture and interface suite and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice,…
objectives: Express accessibility, security, privacy, reliability, performance, modifiability, operability, and cost as stimulus-response-measure scenarios.
Compare modular monolith, service, event, batch, managed, and buy options against explicit quality scenarios and team constraints.
Design runtime-validated APIs or events with compatibility, versioning, idempotency, pagination, error, deprecation, and consumer evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-2 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-2
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Quality Scenario
steps.instruction:
debug case — A team proposes five services without quality scenarios, independent deployment need, compatibility policy, failure containment, or ownership capacity. The debug variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Express accessibility, security, privacy, reliability, performance, modifiability, operability, and cost as stimulus-response-measure scenarios.

Inspect the Quality Scenario trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug Quality Scenario.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for express accessibility, security, privacy, reliability, performance, modifiability, operability, and cost as stimulus-response-measure scenarios. For Inspect the failure record · Debug Quality Scenario, keep Quality Scenario connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: For Quality Scenario, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Inspect the failure record · Debug Quality Scenario, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Quality Scenario precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Inspect the failure record · Debug Quality Scenario, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A team proposes five services without quality scenarios, independent deployment need, compatibility policy, failure containment, or ownership capacity. The debug variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Express accessibility, security, privacy, reliability, performance, modifiability, operability, and cost as stimulus-response-measure scenarios.

For Inspect the failure record · Debug Quality Scenario, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Words like scalable and secure are usable architecture requirements.

stimulus.title: Quality Scenario case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A team proposes five services without quality scenarios, independent deployment need, compatibility policy, failure containment, or ownership capacity. The debug variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Express accessibility, security, privacy, reliability, performance, modifiability, operability, and cost as stimulus-response-measure scenarios.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Words like scalable and secure are usable architecture requirements.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Express accessibility, security, privacy, reliability, performance, modifiability, operability, and cost as stimulus-response-measure scenarios. Artifact under review: quality-attribute architecture and interface suite.

options.text: The Quality Scenario record says quality-attribute architecture and interface suite looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect the failure record evidence boundary for Quality Scenario.

For inspect the failure record, apply Quality Scenario without its stated preconditions, omit the quality-attribute architecture and interface suite trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Quality Scenario for quality-attribute architecture and interface suite.
The record names the assumption, applies Quality Scenario, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Adr Review Trigger
steps.instruction:
Append a pp2-adr-review-trigger-1b81dv: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Adr Review Trigger.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Adr Review Trigger into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Adr Review Trigger, keep Adr Review Trigger connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.code:
pp2-adr-review-trigger-1b81dv:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Adr Review Trigger structural check is only one gate. quality-attribute architecture and interface suite must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

hints: For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Adr Review Trigger, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Adr Review Trigger precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Adr Review Trigger, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A team proposes five services without quality scenarios, independent deployment need, compatibility policy, failure containment, or ownership capacity. The debug variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Attach evidence, consequence, owner, expiry, and measurable review triggers to architecture decisions.

For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Adr Review Trigger, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: An accepted architecture decision should not change during delivery.

steps.title: Order the regression proof · Debug Adr Review Trigger
steps.instruction:
Order the Adr Review Trigger evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

steps.title: Publish the incident record · Debug Adr Review Trigger
steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for Adr Review Trigger: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A team proposes five services without quality scenarios, independent deployment need, compatibility policy, failure containment, or ownership capacity. The debug variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Attach evidence, consequence, owner, expiry, and measurable review triggers to architecture decisions.

Evidence target: Publish the incident record · Debug Adr Review Trigger.
steps.why: Retrieving Adr Review Trigger through the concrete quality-attribute architecture and interface suite case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Publish the incident record · Debug Adr Review Trigger, keep Adr Review Trigger connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Attach evidence, consequence, owner, expiry, and measurable review triggers to architecture decisions.
